page: Vultr vc2-2c-2gb is an entry-level virtual machine within the Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration provides 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core. Memory capacity is limited to 2.0 GB, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 65 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU acceleration is present on this instance type.

Benchmark evaluations position this server in the lower tiers of performance. The shared CPU allocation results in a stress-ng single-core score of 1260.88 and a multi-core score of 2444.49, both categorized in the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Memory performance is similarly constrained, with cached small-block read bandwidth at 177.44 GB/sec, while uncached large-block read and write speeds drop to 12.18 GB/sec and 10.85 GB/sec respectively, placing these memory metrics in the bottom 10%. Passmark CPU and memory scores further confirm this tier, registering at 2453.98 and 938.79.

The primary tradeoff of the vc2-2c-2gb is its shared CPU model and low memory-to-core ratio, which limits its utility for resource-intensive tasks. Database operations are restricted, as reflected in the Passmark database score of 386.17 and Redis SET throughput of 1.39 million operations per second. Static web serving throughput reaches 1.72 GB/sec. Consequently, this server is best suited for lightweight, non-critical workloads such as basic web serving, development environments, small utility scripts, or low-traffic application testing where budget efficiency is prioritized over raw computational throughput.
description: The Vultr vc2-2c-2gb is an entry-level virtual server in the Cloud Compute family, utilizing an x86_64 Intel Xeon processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 2 shared vCPUs, 2.0 GB of system memory, and a bundled 65 GB NVMe SSD. Lacking GPU hardware, this instance is designed for budget-conscious, low-intensity applications. Benchmark data indicates limited performance, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU metrics falling into the bottom 25%, and uncached memory bandwidth dropping to the bottom 10%. Database and web serving benchmarks, such as a Redis SET throughput of 1.39 million operations per second and static web serving at 1.72 GB/sec, reflect its resource constraints. This server is best suited for lightweight workloads, including basic web hosting, development testing, and simple utility scripts where high computational throughput is not required.

2.0 GB of system memory with 1.0 GB per core
